channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says Iran to Allow More Oil Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump characterized the move as a “sign of respect” even as fighting between Israel and Iran continued and the war dragged into its second month.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One on Sunday night.

item.title: Zelenski negocia pactos de defensa aérea con tres países del golfo Pérsico
item.description: El presidente de Ucrania visitó la región, donde intentó transformar a su país de beneficiario a proveedor de ayuda militar.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Servicio de Prensa Presidencial de Ucrania, vía Agence France-Presse
media:content.media:description: En una foto difundida por el Servicio de Prensa Presidencial de Ucrania, el presidente Volodímir Zelenski, de negro, y el emir de Catar, jeque Tamim bin Hamad al-Thani, en el centro a la izquierda, caminan juntos durante su reunión en Doha, Catar, el sábado.

item.title: The Founder of an ‘Orgasmic Meditation’ Group Is in Jail. And at Dinner?
item.description: Nicole Daedone, the former leader of OneTaste, is set to be sentenced after being convicted of forced labor conspiracy last year. But she is still finding ways to spread her message from behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Jackie Molloy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nicole Daedone, who founded the wellness company OneTaste, is potentially facing decades in prison, but she is still finding ways to connect with other women about female empowerment.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 30, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘clemency’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 201 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: clemency
item.description: This word has appeared in 201 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: In Australia, Victoria and Tasmania Waive Transit Fares as Fuel Prices Soar
item.description: The measures in Victoria and Tasmania are temporary, but show how far and how intensely the oil shock from the war in Iran has spread.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Asanka Ratnayake/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Commuters disembark from a train in November in Melbourne, Australia.

item.title: Across Asia, Trump’s War on Iran Is Dangerous for Everyone
item.description: Trump’s war on Iran is dangerous for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Meena Kandasamy

media:content.media:credit: Abhishek Khedekar for The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new iteration of the Bravo franchise begins and the second season of “Your Friends & Neighbors” premieres.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Scott Eisen/Bravo
media:content.media:description: A still from “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island.”

item.title: Iran to Allow More Oil Ships Through Strait of Hormuz, Trump Says
item.description: President Trump said that Iran had agreed to release 20 more cargo ships of oil through the Strait of Hormuz starting on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One on Sunday night.

item.title: EE. UU. permitirá que un petrolero ruso llegue a Cuba y rompa el bloqueo
item.description: El buque con crudo podría arribar el martes y dar un respiro a la isla en medio de la fuerte presión de Washington.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Eric Schmitt

media:content.media:credit: Adalberto Roque/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: La Habana durante un apagón este mes

item.title: Asia Depends on L.N.G. From the Middle East. This Is What Happens When It Runs Out.
item.description: With a cutoff in shipments imminent, Asian countries, the biggest importers of liquefied natural gas from the Middle East, are already burning more coal and reducing consumption.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Dar Yasin/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Residents waiting to receive canisters of household gas this month in Srinagar in Indian-controlled Kashmir.

item.title: He Led Congo for 18 Years. Now, Joseph Kabila Is a Hunted Man.
item.description: Joseph Kabila, the former president, faces the death penalty after the government convicted him of treason last year. He says the charges are bogus.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Guerchom Ndebo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Joseph Kabila returned to the Democratic Republic of Congo last year, to territory controlled by rebels.

item.title: They’ve Been Accused of Running a ‘Covert’ Operation in Greenland. It’s No Secret.
item.description: Members of President Trump’s circle, working in plain sight, have caught the eye of Denmark’s intelligence services for trying to make friends and cut deals on the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Ivor Prickett for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nuuk, Greenland, last year.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Catholic Church Has Surge of Converts in U.S.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, March 30, 2026.

item.title: Gao Zhen, a Chinese Artist Accused of Mocking Mao, Goes on Trial
item.description: Gao Zhen, who emigrated to the United States years ago, was arrested during a visit to China and now faces up to three years in prison for artwork.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Shiho Fukada for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gao Zhen, left, and his brother Gao Qiang, with their artwork, “Mao’s Guilt,” in 2009.

item.title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
item.description: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 2: Getting the Band Back Together
item.description: Valerie doesn’t fully understand why people in Hollywood are so worked up about A.I. She just sees a wave she thinks she knows how to ride.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:credit: Erin Simkin/HBO
media:content.media:description: The director James Burrows, as himself, with Lisa Kudrow, as Valerie Cherish, in a scene from “The Comeback.”

item.title: Trump Says Iran Agreed to Allow 20 More Ships of Oil Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president, speaking to reporters on Air Force One, cast the permission as a “sign of respect.” He also asserted that the United States had already achieved “regime change” in Iran.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Elke Scholiers/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Ships anchored in Muscat, Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz, on Wednesday.

item.title: Oil Jumps to $116 a Barrel on Signs of Escalation of Middle East War
item.description: Investors digested increasing signs of escalation of the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran, which has led to a blockage of critical energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Special Operations Forces Sent to Mideast as Trump Weighs Next Move
item.description: The commandos have not been assigned specific roles. But President Trump has expressed concern about the now-closed Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s store of enriched uranium.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Marines training in Okinawa, Japan, last year. Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit have been sent to the Middle East, though it is unclear what they will be used for.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: Diplomats from around the region met in Pakistan, and the U.S.-Israeli bombardment hit a southern Iranian port, killing at least five people, Iranian state media said.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Airstrikes damaged the Tehran headquarters of Al Araby TV on Sunday.

item.title: Mamdani and Curtis Sliwa Performed a Skit. N.Y. Republicans Are Livid.
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and his former Republican opponent recorded a skit on cat adoption for the Inner Circle, an annual press corps roast. Some politicians weren’t laughing.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times

item.title: 5 conclusiones de las protestas ‘No Kings’ con miras a las elecciones intermedias
item.description: La guerra en Irán impulsó a muchos jóvenes a salir a las calles, pero el control migratorio del gobierno de Trump sigue siendo un tema central. Los candidatos al Senado se unieron a la multitud.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Manifestantes de las marchas “No Kings” el sábado frente al Capitolio del estado de Idaho, en Boise. Se realizaron miles de protestas de distintos tamaños en todo el país.

item.title: Bernie Sanders, in the Bronx, Presses Kathy Hochul to Tax the Rich
item.description: Senator Bernie Sanders, speaking at a rally at Lehman College, lent his support to Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s effort to raise taxes on millionaires. The mayor was not present.
item.dc:creator: Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Charly Triballeau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Senator Bernie Sanders, who has traveled across the United States to argue for higher taxes on the wealthy, spoke to a large crowd at Lehman College in the Bronx on Sunday.

item.title: Nicholas Haysom, Apartheid Foe Who Became a U.N. Peacemaker, Dies at 73
item.description: As an anti-apartheid lawyer, he was jailed several times. Later, he was a top adviser to Nelson Mandela and held key posts at the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:credit: Albin Lohr-Jones/Pacific Press/LightRocket, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Nicholas Haysom at a U.N. Security Council meeting in 2019. The U.N. secretary general, António Guterres, said Mr. Haysom had “devoted his life to justice, dialogue and reconciliation” in “some of the world’s most complex and fragile settings.”

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 30, 2026
item.description: It’s smooth sailing through Gary Cee’s Monday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Reinstein

media:content.media:credit: Ian Forsyth/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: We think this puzzle will cheer you up.

item.title: Politico Names Jonathan Greenberger as New Editor
item.description: Jonathan Greenberger, an executive at the publication, will take over from John Harris, a co-founder of Politico.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Lorenzo Bevilaqua
media:content.media:description: Jonathan Greenberger, who joined Politico in 2024, said in a statement, “My job is to push our journalism further and faster than ever before.”

item.title: U.S. Allows Russian Oil Tanker to Reach Cuba, Despite Blockade
item.description: The tanker full of crude oil could reach its expected destination by Monday, providing a lifeline to the island amid intense U.S. pressure.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Eric Schmitt

media:content.media:credit: Adalberto Roque/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Havana during a blackout this month.

item.title: Israel’s Other War
item.description: As missiles fly in Iran, Israeli troops have moved deep into southern Lebanon to hit Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Israeli airstrikes in Beirut, Lebanon this month.

item.title: Catholic Leaders Barred From Jerusalem Holy Site on Palm Sunday
item.description: Israeli police stopped clergymen from celebrating the Palm Sunday Mass at the Church of the Holy Sepulchre “for the first time in centuries,” church officials said.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Ammar Awad
media:content.media:description: Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa leading a prayer service to mark Palm Sunday in Jerusalem, amid restrictions on gathering in large groups because of the war in Iran.

item.title: As Gas Prices Approach $4 a Gallon, Americans Rethink Vacations
item.description: Already pinched by the high costs of living, some families have modified how they plan to travel by road and air as the Iran war pushes gas and oil prices higher.
item.dc:creator: Audra D. S. Burch
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gas prices in McAllen, Texas, on Friday.

item.title: Australian Sky Turns an Apocalyptic Blood Red
item.description: Winds from Tropical Cyclone Narelle stirred up rust-colored dust from iron-rich soil, tinging the sky over Western Australia with a reddish Mars-like hue.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

item.title: Australia Skies Turn Blood Red Ahead of Tropical Cyclone
item.description: A fiery red sky covered Western Australia as Tropical Cyclone Narelle approached on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: Este astronauta perdió el habla en el espacio. Los doctores aún no saben por qué
item.description: De un momento a otro, Michael Fincke perdió el habla durante una misión en la Estación Espacial Internacional en enero. El episodio provocó una inusual evacuación de regreso a la Tierra.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: NASA/Bill Ingalls , vía Reuters
media:content.media:description: Mike Fincke, astronauta de la NASA, dijo que los médicos no están seguros de cuál fue la causa de su episodio médico, que provocó una evacuación de la Estación Espacial Internacional.

item.title: New Political Group to Push Trump’s A.I. Agenda in Midterms
item.description: The group, Innovation Council Action, says it plans to spend at least $100 million. It will be led by a former administration official.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Taylor Budowich, a longtime political adviser to President Trump, in the Oval Office last year. He will be leading the new Innovation Council.

item.title: Eric Overmyer, Who Wrote for Modern Television Classics, Dies at 74
item.description: Trained as a playwright, he got his first TV writing job on “St. Elsewhere,” then worked on “Homicide: Life on the Street,” “The Wire,” “Treme” and “Bosch.”
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Homicide: Life on the Street (TV Program)

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Eric Overmyer in 1994. “His scripts have a remarkable ability to thrill, enlighten and bemuse,” the writer and television producer Tom Fontana said.

item.title: Iran War Draws Attention to U.S. Troop Presence in Saudi Arabia
item.description: A close U.S. ally and regional rival of Iran, the kingdom has faced hundreds of Iranian missile and drone attacks since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base in Dover, Del., of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, who was killed at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia earlier in March.

item.title: La tecnología debilita nuestra mente. Es posible resistirse
item.description: Deberíamos plantearnos adoptar una postura tan firme contra los contenidos digitales ultraprocesados, en TikTok o Instagram, como la que ya adoptamos contra los alimentos ultraprocesados.
item.dc:creator: Cal Newport

item.title: For NASA’s Artemis II Crew, Journey to the Moon ‘Starting to Feel Real’
item.description: The four astronauts — three Americans and one Canadian — spoke from a prelaunch quarantine ahead of their scheduled Wednesday mission.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: From left, Jeremy Hansen, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman and Victor Glover, members of the Artemis II crew, after arriving at the Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Friday. Mr. Wiseman is holding a plush toy named Rise that will fly in their capsule during their journey to the moon.

item.title: The U.S. Now Has Over 50,000 Troops in the Middle East
item.description: The arrival of 2,500 Marines and another 2,500 sailors is keeping the number of American troops in the region at roughly 10,000 more than usual.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Edgar Su/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The U.S.S. Tripoli entering the Singapore Strait earlier this month. The 2,500 Marines who have been dispatched to the Middle East are part of the U.S.S. Tripoli amphibious ready group.

item.title: Hundreds Gather for Funeral of 3 Lebanese Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: Israel said one of those killed was a member of Hezbollah’s military wing “disguised as a journalist.” Lebanon’s president said all three were journalists and condemned the killings.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Hwaida Saad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times

item.title: ICE May Remain at Airports Even After T.S.A. Pay Resumes, Border Czar Says
item.description: Transportation safety officers are set to be paid on Monday, but Tom Homan, the White House’s border czar, said ICE agents may stay where there are shortages.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tom Homan contended on Sunday that wait times had decreased at airports since ICE agents arrived, doing identification checks and “plugging the security holes.”

item.title: Can Canada’s Left Regroup? A New Leader Will Try.
item.description: Seeking a comeback after voters flocked to Mark Carney’s Liberals, the New Democrats picked Avi Lewis, a scion of a leftist political dynasty.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:credit: Shannon Vanraes/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Avi Lewis, center, after his election on Sunday as the leader of Canada’s New Democratic Party at the party convention in Winnipeg, Canada.

item.title: Make Melissa Clark’s Lemon Poppy Seed Cake
item.description: The olive oil in this five-star recipe keeps the cake from drying out, which means you can snack on it all week long.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Ko

media:content.media:credit: Dane Tashima for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s lemon poppy seed pound cake.

item.title: The Death Penalty’s Invisible Wounds
item.description: Readers respond to an editorial about the death penalty. Also: The benefits of small talk; calling all teenagers.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Rebecca Chew/The New York Times

item.title: Your Chatbot Isn’t a Therapist
item.description: Patients are turning to A.I. chatbots for therapy — and the chatbots are fueling their worst impulses.
item.dc:creator: Divya Saini and Natasha Bailen

item.title: Confronting the Chaos
item.description: We offer some suggestions to help you get started on spring cleaning.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright-Piersanti

media:content.media:credit: Winnie Au for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Says Iran Agreed to Allow 20 More Ships of Oil Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president characterized Iran’s permission as a “sign of respect.” Israel said it launched a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure in Tehran and other areas, and Iran retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
